title: Selma Lagerlöf | History | Research Starters | EBSCO Research
content: As the first woman to receive the Nobel Prize in Literature in 1909, she is celebrated for her unique blend of realism and magical elements in storytelling. Her breakthrough novel, "Gösta Berlings Saga," published in 1891, features a group of colorful characters and explores themes of freedom and responsibility, laying the groundwork for her literary reputation. Throughout her career, Lagerlöf wrote a variety of works, including novels, short stories, and children's literature, with "The Wonderful Adventures of Nils" becoming particularly popular. Her writing often incorporated local folklore and reflected the complexities of human nature, merging the ordinary with the extraordinary. Her works continue to resonate with readers worldwide, highlighting her belief in the importance of myths and traditions in understanding human experience. During her lifetime, she was loved throughout the world because of her gift for storytelling and her idealism, which was a welcome change from the pessimistic realism dominating the time.
